article_title: Humanoid and Schaeffler Sign Binding Deal to Deploy a Four-Digit Fleet of Wheeled Humanoid Robots Across Global Factories by 2032
summary: Submission approvable with 3 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.details:
therobotreport.com: https://www.therobotreport.com/schaeffler-humanoid-partner-build-deploy-hundreds-robots/
dcvelocity.com: https://www.dcvelocity.com/material-handling/robotics/schaeffler-to-use-thousands-of-humanoid-robots-in-auto-parts-factories
unite.ai: https://www.unite.ai/humanoid-secures-major-manufacturing-deal-with-schaeffler-to-deploy-thousands-of-robots/
roboticstomorrow.com: https://www.roboticstomorrow.com/news/2026/05/13/humanoid-secures-landmark-deal-with-schaeffler-to-deploy-thousands-of-humanoid-robots/26562/
humanoidroboticstechnology.com: https://humanoidroboticstechnology.com/industry-news/humanoid-to-deploy-thousands-of-humanoid-robots-on-schaeffler-factory-floors/
siliconcanals.com: https://siliconcanals.com/uks-humanoid-unveils-hmnd-01/

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively excellent submission. Every key specific verified verbatim across multiple accessible sources: May 13 announcement, binding-phased framing, four-digit target by 2032, five-year actuator supply through 2031, seven-digit cumulative actuator volume, Robot-as-a-Service structure, Herzogenaurach 'advancing box handling' use case verbatim, Schweinfurt testing phase, December 2026–June 2027 initial deployment, January 2026 strategic partnership lineage. Both direct quotes — Sokolov's 'together with Schaeffler, one of our key industrial partners...' and Schroeder's 'By supporting the phased deployment... future-oriented motion solutions' — verbatim. HMND 01 hardware specs (175 cm / 70 kg / 1.5 m/s / 4 hours / 15 kg payload, founded 2024 in London by Sokolov) verbatim in Silicon Canals.

source-0.status: The Robot Report (Archive.org snapshot). Verifies: RaaS framing, 'preferred supplier' for wheeled-platform joint actuators, wheeled-line scope. Note: snapshot was captured via Archive.org because live URL returned 200 to the scraper but content differed.

source-1.status: DC Velocity. Verifies: December 2026 → June 2027 initial phase, seven-digit cumulative actuator volume, wheeled + bipedal lineup, 'thousands' framing.

source-2.status: Interesting Engineering. Verifies: 'one of the largest disclosed humanoid robot rollouts to date' verbatim, four-digit by 2032, Schweinfurt testing phase, Schroeder full quote verbatim, binding-phased framing, five-year supply through 2031.

source-3.status: BOT-BLOCKED (HTTP 403). Article cites this for the 'joint actuators can account for roughly 50% of a humanoid robot's bill of materials' claim. This specific framing is exclusively in unite.ai per the bot's research log; the underlying preferred-supplier-for-50%-of-joint-actuators contract structure IS verified elsewhere (s2, s4) — the cost-of-BOM percentage is the one Unite.AI-exclusive claim.

source-4.status: Robotics Tomorrow. Verifies: May 13 dateline, binding-phased framing, four-digit by 2032, 'advancing box handling use case within a live production environment' verbatim, Herzogenaurach focus, Sokolov full quote verbatim, more-than-50% preferred-supplier scope, five-year through 2031, seven-digit actuator volume, January 2026 strategic-partnership lineage.

source-5.status: BOT-BLOCKED (HTTP 403). Article cites this for the January 2026 strategic-partnership / proof-of-concept lineage — both confirmed independently in source-4.

source-6.status: Silicon Canals. Verifies: full HMND 01 hardware specs (175 cm, 70 kg, 1.5 m/s, 4-hour runtime, 15 kg payload), 2024 founding date, London HQ, Sokolov-as-founder.

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent structure (Overview / What We Know with five sub-sections / What We Don't Know / Analysis). Neutral, professional tone. Length appropriate for News (1064 words). The 'What We Don't Know' section honestly enumerates the missing numbers (contract value, RaaS subscription price, plant headcount targets, productivity metrics).
editor_notes.originality: Genuine new development on an ongoing story — correctly cross-references the April 28 Hannover Messe article for Humanoid's prior coverage.
concerns: Two cited sources (unite.ai, humanoidroboticstechnology.com) returned HTTP 403. The Unite.AI-exclusive claim — that joint actuators 'can account for roughly 50% of a humanoid robot's bill of materials' — is editorial framing not independently verifiable in the accessible source set. The underlying contract specifics (preferred-supplier for >50% of joint actuators) ARE in s2 and s4.
recommendations: Add therobotreport.com, dcvelocity.com, unite.ai, roboticstomorrow.com, humanoidroboticstechnology.com, siliconcanals.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all recurring robotics-industry outlets.

content_preview.title: Humanoid and Schaeffler Sign Binding Deal to Deploy a Four-Digit Fleet of Wheeled Humanoid Robots Across Global Factories by 2032
content_preview.summary: A May 13 deal pairs a Robot-as-a-Service deployment running through 2032 with a five-year actuator supply agreement that makes Schaeffler the preferred supplier for more than half of Humanoid's joint actuators.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

UK-based humanoid robotics startup Humanoid and German motion-technology group Schaeffler announced on May 13 a binding, phased deployment and supply agreement that combines two contracts: a Robot-as-a-Service rollout that targets a four-digit number of wheeled humanoid units across Schaeffler's global factories by 2032, and a five-year actuator supply deal that makes Schaeffler the preferred supplier for more than half of Humanoid's joint actuators through 2031, [according to Robot...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
